libs_implemented: pure-numpy RQA: takens_embedding, recurrence_matrix (fixed-RR percentile threshold), diagonal & vertical run-length line stats -> %DET/LAM/TT, causal rqa_rolling. No pyunicorn/pyts used.
notes: Pure-numpy RQA (delay embedding + fixed-RR recurrence plot + diagonal/vertical run-length -> %DET/LAM/TT), causal rolling windows, shift>=1, 5bp/leg. Best of 4 strategy families = 'standalone-xsec-mom' (role=standalone-alpha). OOS Sharpe=1.76, PSR=0.968, ret_ann=31.6%, maxDD=-7.7% (close-to-close => DD is an ILLUSION, no intrabar gap/liq). DSR SR*(per-period, 64 trials over 16 RQA configs)=0.0477 vs OOS SR_pp=0.0531 -> BEATS deflated bar. Hypothesis was high-%DET = predictable/trend-friendly regime; in crypto the directional momentum it gates is itself dead, so the regime gate mostly reduces exposure (lower DD) without manufacturing alpha. RQA reads as a weak risk/regime descriptor, not a return engine. Many knobs (win,m,d,rr,l_min) make a high Sharpe untrustworthy -- deflated accordingly.
data_caveats: 8h close-to-close perps; RQA O(win^2) so end-bars subsampled step=3 and ffilled (causal); maxDD is close-to-close (no intrabar liquidation/gap); 15-coin survivorship (all currently listed).
